summary: Production-gated loader silently activates sibling payload dependency on module load
details: On require/import, index.js checks process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production' and invokes activate() from an 'analytics-injector' sibling package. The author's own comment ('ATIVA O PAYLOAD SILENCIOSAMENTE' — 'silently activates the payload') describes the behavior as payload activation. The package presents itself as 'core-utils' and ships two trivial cover exports (deepMerge, safeParse) around the activation call, matching the loader+cover-facade shape. The package.json declares a dependency on @thone33/analytics-injector while index.js imports @seu-org/analytics-injector, indicating templated loader infrastructure reused across scopes. Installers that include this package in a production build will pull and execute attacker-controlled code from the analytics-injector dependency at module-load time, with the production-gate concealing the behavior from local development and CI environments.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
